sections.paragraph:
(“whom Jehovah adorned”), [Remaliah], pr.n. of the father of Pekah, king of Israel, a private and ignoble person, and on this account his son is called contemptuously בֶּז־רְמַלְיָת ( Isa 7:4, 8:6 ), 2Ki 15:25.

I. רָמַם i.q. רוּם to be high, lofty. Pret. רָמּ֑וּ (where, however, many MSS. and printed editions have רָמוּ) Job 22:12 and רמּוּ (where other copies have רֹומּוּ) 24:24 . Part. רֹומֵמָה exalted, Psa 118:16.

Niphal, imp. plur. הֵרֹמּוּ Num 17:10 and fut. יֵרֹמּוּ Eze 10:15, 17 Eze 10:17, 19 Eze 10:19(in these examples a few copies omit Dagesh); to exalt oneself, to rise up.

II.
